strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Makes the scheduled phase loop rely on CI-enforced guard behavior before unattended PR merge decisions.
Keeps future runtime permission and mutation work behind a machine-checked value gate instead of advisory review notes.
Removes the guard-enforcement blocker before Brownie begins the first controlled workspace mutation phase.

questions.question: Which strategic capabilities does this phase advance?
What new user-visible, operator-visible, or runtime capability does it add?
Why can existing functionality not substitute for this work?
What concrete product gap remains if this is not implemented?
Is this semantically distinct from the previous three phases?
Can this be done without adding a new RPC?
Can this be done without adding a new summary surface?
What runtime or development-control behavior actually changes?
How does this help headless autonomous development?
How does this move the milestone toward completion?

answers.strategic_capabilities: Advances headless_autonomous_development and supports runtime_permission_enforcement plus controlled_workspace_tools by making the scheduled phase loop enforce guardrails in CI.
answers.new_capability: The existing VSIX check CI path can reject PRs that skip diagnostics regression checks, skip phase-value checks, or change guard engine files without explicit strict review metadata.
answers.why_existing_functionality_is_insufficie: The existing CI path already invokes the guard scripts through the VSIX check step, but the phase-value guard is hard-coded to M5.38 instead of the current manifest and guard engine changes are not review-visible.
answers.product_gap_if_skipped: The loop can enter M6 controlled workspace mutation work without machine-enforced protection against wrapper-only phases or stale phase manifests.
answers.non_duplicative: Yes. M5.36-M5.38 added runtime inspection and consumed-join recovery behavior; R2.1 changes repository CI guard enforcement and current manifest validation.
answers.no_new_rpc: Yes. The capability is repository guard execution and does not require any runtime RPC.
answers.no_new_summary: Yes. The capability is enforced by CI and guard tests, not by adding another runtime summary.
answers.runtime_behavior_change: Brownie runtime behavior does not change in this phase; scheduled development-control behavior changes because guard execution becomes a PR gate.
answers.headless_autonomous_contribution: An unattended controller can rely on CI to enforce value and diagnostics guardrails before review or merge decisions.
answers.milestone_progress: This removes the guard activation blocker before M6 Controlled Apply Execution begins.

reject_when: The PR does not preserve or demonstrate the existing CI execution path for both guard families.
The phase-value guard remains tied to M5.38 or another historical phase.
The current manifest cannot be selected deterministically.
Guard engine changes lack strict review metadata.
The PR adds a diagnostics wrapper, runtime RPC, ledger event, VSIX protocol surface, controlled apply behavior, or product implementation outside guard activation.

guard_engine_change_review.review_intent: R2.1 changes the guard engine and current phase manifest while relying on the existing VSIX check CI path, so review must confirm the gate is stricter, generic, and still transitively enforced before merge.

exit_criteria: Existing GitHub Actions CI executes pnpm --filter brownie-vsix check.
extensions/brownie-vsix package check executes pnpm --workspace-root guard:diagnostics.
extensions/brownie-vsix package check executes pnpm --workspace-root guard:phase-value.
Local validation executes phase-value guard behavior tests.
scripts/guard-phase-value.mjs validates docs/architecture/phase-value-manifest.json as the deterministic current manifest.
scripts/guard-phase-value.mjs contains no M5.38-specific phase or manifest constant.
Guard engine file changes require guard_engine_change_review metadata.
GitHub Actions shallow pull request checkouts fetch the base ref and fall back to base-tree diff when three-dot diff has no merge base.
Behavior tests prove generic non-M5 manifest validation.
Behavior tests prove guard engine change detection, including shallow pull request checkout fallback.

No diagnostics wrapper, runtime RPC, ledger event, VSIX protocol surface, controlled apply implementation, scheduler, child auto-run, process execution, network, service control, or patch apply capability is added.

test_tokens: validates a non-M5 current manifest without source-token hard-coding
requires explicit review metadata when guard engine files change
detects guard engine changes from GitHub Actions shallow pull request checkouts
